pg1-p2-s1:
War fever ran high in the New England town to which we new, young officers from Plattsburg were assigned, and we were
flattered when the first citizens took us to their homes, making us feel heroic.
pg1-p2-s2:
Here was love, applause, war; moments
sublime with intervals hilarious.
pg1-p2-s3: I was part of life at last, and in the midst of the excitement I discovered liquor.
pg1-p2-s4:
I
forgot the strong warnings and the prejudices of my people concerning drink.
pg1-p2-s5: In time we sailed for "Over There."
pg1-p2-s6:
I was
very lonely and again turned to alcohol.
pg1-p2:
War fever ran high in the New England town to which we new, young officers from Plattsburg were assigned, and we were
flattered when the first citizens took us to their homes, making us feel heroic. Here was love, applause, war; moments
sublime with intervals hilarious. I was part of life at last, and in the midst of the excitement I discovered liquor. I
forgot the strong warnings and the prejudices of my people concerning drink. In time we sailed for "Over There." I was
very lonely and again turned to alcohol.
pg1-p3-s4:
My attention was caught by a
doggerel on an old tombstone:
pg1-p3:
We landed in England. I visited Winchester Cathedral. Much moved, I wandered outside. My attention was caught by a
doggerel on an old tombstone:
pg1-p4-s1:
"Here lies a Hampshire Grenadier
Who caught his death
Drinking cold small beer.
A good soldier is ne'er forgot
Whether he dieth by musket
Or by pot."
pg1-p4:
"Here lies a Hampshire Grenadier
Who caught his death
Drinking cold small beer.
A good soldier is ne'er forgot
Whether he dieth by musket
Or by pot."
pg1-p5-s1: Ominous warning - which I failed to heed.
pg1-p5: Ominous warning - which I failed to heed.
pg1-p6-s1: Twenty-two, and a veteran of foreign wars, I went home at last.
pg1-p6-s2:
I fancied myself a leader, for had not the men of my
battery given me a special token of appreciation?
pg1-p6-s3:
My talent for leadership, I imagined, would place me at the head of
vast enterprises which I would manage with the utmost assurance.
pg1-p6:
Twenty-two, and a veteran of foreign wars, I went home at last. I fancied myself a leader, for had not the men of my
battery given me a special token of appreciation? My talent for leadership, I imagined, would place me at the head of
vast enterprises which I would manage with the utmost assurance.
